mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and subsequently S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.3pp over 3 months, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also elevated, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment that stifles economic growth and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are above their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, as indicated by robust GDP expansion, healthy demand translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no significant outperformance during periods of GDPC1 growth above 2% annualized compared to other regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector, indicating broader economic weakness that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following a month-over-month INDPRO decline exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), indicates a healthy labor market supporting consumer confidence and spending, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exhibit a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are declining while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, it indicates a tightening monetary policy impacting a crucial demand sector, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average change < 0 AND FEDFUNDS 3-month average change > 0
regime_hint: housing market downturn with monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where HOUST is declining and FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, a stable or declining Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals accommodative monetary conditions, reducing the cost of capital for businesses and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns do not show significantly higher performance during periods of low inflation and stable/declining FEDFUNDS compared to other regimes.
